serviceNotes.engine oil: Mfr row: W211 petrol models on the common European fixed maintenance plan specify engine oil and filter at 15,000 km or 12 months (oil-change.info and workshop summaries for W211 petrol). Rec row: 10,000 km is a conservative interval for turbocharged M271/M272 use in severe conditions.
serviceNotes.dust filter: Mercedes-Benz dust filter (cabin filter) replacement per EU maintenance booklet (typically every 24 months and/or 30,000 km with major services).
serviceNotes.engine air filter: Engine air filter follows Mercedes inspection mileage items (commonly 60,000 km or 48 months).
serviceNotes.brake fluid: Mercedes-Benz specifies brake fluid replacement every 24 months; time-based only per operator manual guidance.
serviceNotes.check brake pad thickness: Brake pad inspection at each scheduled service; 30,000 km mid-cycle reminder for high annual mileage.
serviceNotes.spark plugs: Spark plugs for M272/M273 V6 and related petrol engines are renewed at extended intervals on EU sheets (commonly 60,000 km or 48 months—confirm for your engine code).
serviceNotes.coolant: Long-life coolant exchange appears in Mercedes maintenance tables around 150,000 km for this generation; verify printed service booklet.
serviceNotes.automatic transmission fluid: 722.6/722.9 units are often lifetime-fill officially; ATF service near 60,000 km is a widely used preventive interval.
serviceNotes.manual transmission oil: Manual gearbox oil typically long-life; renewal near 100,000 km is a common workshop recommendation.
serviceNotes.transfer case oil: 4MATIC models only. Preventive fluid renewal around 60,000 km when no OEM routine interval is listed.
serviceNotes.front axle differential oil: 4MATIC models only. Coordinate with transfer case maintenance.
serviceNotes.rear axle differential oil: Preventive differential oil renewal around 60,000 km complements axle wear on AWD variants.
serviceNotes.coolant flush: Full coolant exchange around 120,000 km restores inhibitors if the OEM long interval has not yet occurred.
serviceNotes.poly-V belt: Accessory belt and tensioner near 100,000 km reduces risk of sudden ancillary failure.
serviceNotes.intake carbon clean: Direct-injection petrol engines (e.g. some CGI variants) can accumulate intake valve deposits; walnut or chemical cleaning around 120,000 km restores airflow where symptoms appear.
